creator: pascal.guitierrez@gmail.com
text:
I can reliably reproduce a freeze using iozone on 14.0-RELEASE-p5 using a simple ZFS pool backed onto SATA HDDs as such:

NAME         SIZE  ALLOC   FREE  CKPOINT  EXPANDSZ   FRAG    CAP  DEDUP    HEALTH  ALTROOT
tank        1.81T   512G  1.31T        -         -    36%    27%  1.00x    ONLINE  -
  mirror-0  1.81T   512G  1.31T        -         -    36%  27.6%      -    ONLINE
    ada0    1.82T      -      -        -         -      -      -      -    ONLINE
    ada1    1.82T      -      -        -         -      -      -      -    ONLINE


To reproduce I create a ZFS filesystem with compression=off, then run iozone -i 0,1 -l 512 -r 4k -s 1g

The write tests execute fine (writing out 512 1g files), however when it runs the read tests (1=read/re-read) the system freezes due to an apparent low memory condition after only a few minutes.

I've tried increasing the following sysctls from their defaults:
vm.v_free_target
vm.v_free_min
vm.v_free_severe
vm.v_free_reserved
vfs.zfs.arc.sys_free
vfs.zfs.arc.free_target

and reducing the following:
vfs.zfs.arc_max

But no effect, free memory reliably drops below 100M and does not recover and soon after the system will freeze and require a hard reset.


This machine has 16GB of RAM with no swap (have also tried with swap on zvol but same effect), dmesg is below:
